name: Owlsbury
overview: Owslebury is a village and civil parish about 5 miles south-east of Winchester in Hampshire, set on a hilltop along a former Roman road and within easy reach of the South Downs National Park, with a population of around 818. It is a geographically concentrated village strung along its main road, with a 14th-century parish church (St Andrew's), a 17th-century inn, a well-regarded primary school and a cricket ground in use for roughly 150 years. Marwell Zoo lies towards the south of the parish.
character: A friendly, hilltop downland village with a strong community association, a historic pub and church, and excellent walking on the Pilgrims' and Monarch's Way.
amenities: The Ship Inn (17th-century pub serving food)
Village hall (with part-time Post Office service)
Village green and cricket/football ground (~150 years)

schools.name: The Westgate School and Kings' School (secondary), Winchester

prices.summary: A sought-after village close to Winchester; period and detached homes with strong demand for the school catchment and South Downs setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walking the Pilgrims' Trail and Monarch's Way (60+ footpaths in the parish)
Cricket on the historic village green
Community events at the Glebe Field
placesToEat: Eateries in nearby Winchester and Colden Common
pros: Good-rated primary school and strong community association
Excellent walking and South Downs proximity
Close to Winchester and Marwell Zoo
cons: Only a part-time Post Office and no full shop in the village
No railway station; car-dependent with infrequent buses
whoItSuits: Families wanting a hilltop downland village with a good primary school, historic pub and great walking, close to Winchester.
primarySupermarket: Part-time PO at village hall; supermarkets in Winchester
